exercises.instructions:
Modify the starter code so your program prints three things about you — one per line.

1. Print a greeting with your name
2. Print where you're from (city, state, or whatever feels right)
3. Print one thing you want to learn this year — be honest
4. Run your code with Ctrl+Enter and read the output before doing anything else
5. Click Commit and write a message that describes what your program does — not just 'done'

A good commit message: 'added my name, city, and learning goal'
A bad commit message: 'done'

Common mistakes to avoid:
- Forgetting quotes around your text → NameError
- Mismatched quotes (opening with " and closing with ') → SyntaxError
- Not reading the output before committing — check what actually printed
exercises.starter_code:
# Exercise 0.1 — Your first commit
# Replace the placeholder text with your own information

print("Hello! My name is YOUR NAME HERE.")
print("I'm from YOUR CITY, STATE.")
print("This year I want to learn: YOUR GOAL HERE.")
